perturbation.original_text: AVDU hereby retains UTK to provide those services as defined herein and UTK hereby agrees to the appointment on the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth and agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts in providing said services.
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU hereby retains UTK to provide those services as defined herein and UTK hereby agrees to the appointment on the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth and agrees to dedicate its best efforts in providing said services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that UTK agrees to use 'commercially reasonable efforts' in providing services. The modification changes this to 'dedicate its best efforts.' This introduces an ambiguity because 'commercially reasonable efforts' suggests a cost-benefit analysis, whereas 'dedicate its best efforts' implies a higher standard of performance, potentially requiring UTK to expend more resources than what might be commercially reasonable. This change creates a contradiction with Section III.A where it states that UTK shall devote such time and efforts, as it deems commercially reasonable, under the circumstances to the affairs of the AVDU.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UTK shall devote such time and efforts, as it deems commercially reasonable, under the circumstances to the affairs of the AVDU, as is reasonable and adequate to render the Services contemplated by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'commercially reasonable efforts,' while the changed text mandates 'dedicate its best efforts.' This creates a contradiction because 'best efforts' implies a higher standard than 'commercially reasonable efforts,' potentially requiring UTK to expend more resources. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that UTK shall devote such time and efforts as it deems 'commercially reasonable.' The modified text therefore imposes a higher standard of performance than what is deemed commercially reasonable in the contradicted section, creating a direct inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: AVDU agrees that for a twenty four months (24) following the execution of this Agreement, AVDU shall not, without UTEK's prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for employment any present employee of UTEK, or request induce or advise any employee of UTEK to leave the employ of UTEK. In turn, UTEK agrees that it will not directly or indirectly solicit any present employee of AVDU.
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU agrees that for a twelve months (12) following the execution of this Agreement, AVDU shall not, without UTEK's prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for employment any present employee of UTEK, or request induce or advise any employee of UTEK to leave the employ of UTEK. In turn, UTEK agrees that it will not directly or indirectly solicit any present employee of AVDU.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a 24-month restriction on soliciting employees. This modification changes this restriction to 12 months. This creates a contradiction with item viii of the Confidential Term Sheet (Exhibit A), which specifies a 24-month restriction on acquiring technologies presented by UTK directly from the technology developer. The contradiction arises because both clauses now refer to different durations following the agreement's termination. UTK and AVDU may have an internal contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: AVDU will not seek to acquire any technologies presented to AVDU by UTK directly from the technology developer for a period of 24 months following the termination of this Strategic Alliance agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 24-month restriction on soliciting employees, while the contradicted text refers to a 24-month restriction on acquiring technologies. The modification changes the employee solicitation restriction to 12 months. This creates a direct contradiction because one clause now refers to a 12-month duration and the other to a 24-month duration following the agreement's termination. The change introduces ambiguity regarding which time frame applies to restrictions following the agreement, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
